items.correct: Each new DNA molecule contains one original (parental) strand and one newly synthesised strand.
items.misconception: One daughter molecule contains both original strands; the other contains both new strands.
items.random_wrong: DNA replication produces two identical fully new molecules with no original material.
items.why: Students confuse semi-conservative with conservative replication — they don't grasp that each daughter molecule is a hybrid of old and new strands, as Meselson-Stahl demonstrated.

items.q: Where does transcription happen in a eukaryotic cell, and what is the product?
items.correct: In the nucleus — DNA is copied into mRNA, which then travels to the cytoplasm for translation.
items.misconception: Transcription happens at ribosomes, where DNA is copied directly into protein.
items.random_wrong: Transcription happens in the mitochondria during respiration.
items.why: Students conflate transcription (DNA→mRNA, in the nucleus) with translation (mRNA→protein, at ribosomes); they think it's all one process at the ribosome.

items.q: Where does transcription occur in a eukaryotic cell?
items.correct: In the nucleus — DNA is transcribed to mRNA, which then travels to the cytoplasm.
items.misconception: Transcription happens on ribosomes in the cytoplasm.
items.random_wrong: Transcription happens in mitochondria where ATP is produced.
items.why: Students mix transcription and translation — translation happens on ribosomes, transcription happens in the nucleus.

items.correct: Each new molecule has 1 old and 1 new strand
items.misconception: Each new molecule has 2 old strands
items.random_wrong: Each new molecule has 2 new strands
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Each new molecule has 1 old and 1 new strand. “Each new molecule has 2 old strands” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
The answer examiners want is: DNA ligase. “Helicase” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Starting from one DNA molecule, how many DNA molecules are present after 3 rounds of replication?
items.why: The real figure is 8. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
